location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: Today was my first time trying San Blas food in Lincoln Park, and after waiting an hour for the seafood tower, the waitress arrived with the bill and forgot about the tower. She brought my son two carne asada tacos instead of chicken, and we got four seafood empanadas and two Russian-style empanadas. The food was good, but the service was terrible. We spoke with the manager, and he gave us the food for free because of the misunderstanding, but honestly, I don't think we'll be back. I was very disappointed. We were the only people eating there, and they couldn't give us proper service.

review_text: Everything was great 👍

review_text: The portions are very small yet pricey. The place was clean. They charge you $3 for extra onion. $1 for spicy sauce. The beans you get you can actually scoop up in two bites. But they spread it to look like its a lot. I ordered a big burrito and it looked like a enchilada. Doubt I come back.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: I’ve long been a fan of san Blas glad I no longer have to always go to Detroit now we have a downriver location. I do not know how it is if you go in I ordered take out and I was very disappointed the price I paid for for what I got was absolutely a joke. I ordered a large cocktail de Camerones y pulpo there was 6 shrimp in the cocktail, $35.00 for 6 shrimp and octopus in a cold  tomatoe broth. I also ordered the Camerónes zarandeados there was 7 shrimp on the plate paid $24 for 7 shrimp, a very small side of salad, small portion of rice, very small portion of a variantion of potato salad and a piece of bread. I know for a fact when i dine in at the san Blas in Detroit you get more food! I get those same things all the time down there and the portion sizes are very different. Everything was very good l, good flavor but the portion size for what you pay is ridiculous

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text:
This place is pretty good. I've eaten and drank here multiple times now and thought it was decent. The atmosphere is really cool. It is a little pricey though.

The first couple of times visiting here I just drank. I ordered the pina colada and it tasted very good but it was $11 if I remember correctly. So the mixed drinks and cocktails are little pricey. Just keep that in mind.

With that said, the food is a bit pricey also, specifically their seafood. If you go during lunchtime, their regular non-seafood dishes are priced reasonably. Also, their happy hour beer prices aren't too expensive either.

While the seafood here is quite expensive, the quality is pretty good. I ordered the shrimp quesadilla, as it was one of the most affordable seafood items in the menu. The cheese wasn't that good but the shrimp was excellent. I did get the squid the last time I was here and I can honestly say that it wasn't that good and the portion was very small for the price.

As for their regular dishes (non-seafood), they're okay. Their cheese isn't that good though. It kind of reminds me of cheese wiz.

I do like the variety of sauces that they have. When we first started going, they would give us complimentary ceviche. The last time we were in though, they did not. They said we could order it as an appetizer. I don't know if that was a promotional thing or what. Either way, it was appreciated and it was pretty good.

The atmosphere is pretty cool in here also, as before mentioned. The walls and ceiling are painted blue and there are paintings of fish. It feels like you are under the sea.

Overall, this place is decent. It's quite pricey for their seafood dishes but it is high quality. There regular dishes are okay. It's probably best to go during happy hour if you plan to drink. The decor of this place is quite vibrant also.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: I brought my mom for lunch and this is our first visit and WOW!!!! The menu is enormous and everything looks so delicious!! The fish tacos are out of this world and the BEST I’ve ever had and that says a lot!!! Their drink specials are great too, we sipped on a $4 Margarita, which was so good!! Chips and Salsa are a huge selling point for us too and my gosh, it was so good!!!! The portion sizes are enormous!! We’ll definitely be back!!! Our waitress was amazing and so attentive!! Thank you for a great first experience!!!

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text:
UPDATE: We've been to Mi Lindo San Blas several times now. The last time, they seemed to have had an off night.  The seviche was completely bland. It seemed fresh enough, but definitely not marinated in lime juice. I would also stay away from their oysters. They were not fresh at all and had a terrible taste... I couldn't even swallow it. Brought it up to the waitress, and she mentioned others have said the same. I really hope they take action to correct these issues. Otherwise, we will likely not be back.

ORIGINAL REVIEW: This was our first time visiting Mi Lindo San Blas. Upon entering, you're greeted by lovely murals all over the walls. The space is fairly small but inviting. We were seated promptly, and the waitress immediately took our drink order. We ordered a Long Island and a Michelada, which were a bit pricey ($9.99) but delicious.

For appetizer, we got the shrimp seviche. It was very fresh and delicious... finally, I have a go-to for my seviche cravings! For the main course, my partner got a beef burrito, and I got a whole red snapper Nayarit style. The burrito was very good, but that snapper stole the show! The skin was crispy, but the meat was flakey and tender. Nayarit style sauce added a lot of flavor and just a hint of heat... very tasty. The freshness of the food was apparent, and I hope this never changes.

All in all, a great experience. The food and the ambiance actually reminded me of being back in Mexico.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: Went to the restaurant around 4pm. Two hostesses were working. One waitress was eating at the bar. Two other waitresses were on the floor. There was only three tables that had customers including our table. We were seated with menus. We were never given even water, no chip & salsa.  Two man were with a customer that later turned into this person filling out an application. We sat for at least 10 to 12 mins. No one waited on us. Do not go here. The place wasn’t even busy. Poor customer service. They won’t be in business very long.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text:
They opened about 3 months ago, according to the waitress. The salsa was amazing. It's a little spicy for my kids, but it's great for adults. Food was great for the most part. My taco shells were a little stale, but I dumped everything on the fresh chips and it was great. My husband ordered the botana and said it was amazing. My son ordered chicken fingers and fries. He liked it. My daughter ordered chips and cheese. We think they put the cheese queso on it, which was a bit spicy. "Mouth on fire " lol but she finished them later that night. The margaritas were also delicious!

Their parking lot is very small, so we almost parked on the street, but someone left. Not a huge deal.

review_text: My family and I went for dinner tonight. Let me tell you if you haven’t been here yet you need to go! Support local businesses people. The drinks were delicious, the salsa, the ceviche and our seafood dishes were AMAZING. The pork carnitas left a lot to be desired but the rice and beans were great! location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: I visited San Blas for the first time this week, encouraged by a Kroger coupon, but I won’t be returning. I ordered the Speedy Gonzalez, requesting a ground beef taco, but it came with chicken instead, despite the waitress checking multiple times. My son had the taco lunch and fried ice cream, yet our bill was $30.34, which seemed high. The food wasn’t impressive, the vibe felt empty, and the overall experience lacked a pleasant atmosphere.

review_text: Ordered two micheladas to go finished them in 3 sips since the girl filled both cups with ice and prepared them herself and I’m sure she just used one beer for both. location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text:
I ordered 9 birria tacos and the total came out to almost $60 which is already wild, but I still gave it a chance based on the reviews. I ended up extremely disappointed. The cheese they use for the birria is definitely not real cheese and whatever it is, it should NOT be on birria tacos. I couldn’t even enjoy them ordered 9 and didn’t even like the first one.
On top of that I asked for 4 sides of sour cream and they charged me $7 just for that. Completely overpriced, especially considering I didn’t even end up touching the other 3.

So it was basically a Salad!
First and Last Time going!

Go to Any Other Mexican Restaurant in Lincoln Park.
How can you not get a taco right 🤷‍♂️

Edit: come to think about it, I think I got scammed. It said "(premium)" on the receipt next to the drink, however no premium alcohol was ordered.

FYI don't get the Margarona unless you're ready to spend $25 for one drink. It's the exact same thing as a coronarita anywhere else ($14 Max). There are no prices next to the drinks, and we were surprised to find out a basic margarita with a mini corona was $25! Outrageous! There was no specialty liquor used in the drink either.

review_text: This is my second time here, and honestly, I'd rather talk to the manager than leave reviews here. But this is mainly for the staff who work there. From the moment my children and I walked in, we greeted the person at the entrance. I said, "Hello, good afternoon," and they ignored me. I thought, "Maybe they didn't hear us, okay." Then we got the waitress, and we said, "Hello, good afternoon," and she ignored us again. After that, I took our food and didn't come back except when it was time to pay. The service was terrible, but the food was very good.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text:
From the moment you arrive at Mi Lindo San Blas, it’s clear this is more than just another Mexican restaurant — it’s an experience built around atmosphere, hospitality, and authentic flavor. The exterior immediately stands out with vibrant character and thoughtful architectural detail, while the interior delivers an immersive dining environment that feels warm, energetic, and genuinely transportive. Every aspect of the décor appears intentionally designed to create an inviting and memorable atmosphere, and the restaurant succeeds effortlessly in doing so.

One of the first touches that elevated the experience was the complimentary chips and salsa. While many restaurants treat this as a routine starter, Mi Lindo San Blas delivers freshness and flavor that immediately set the tone for the meal ahead. It’s a small detail, but one that reflects the kitchen’s attention to quality from the very beginning.

The menu itself is impressively expansive, offering an enormous variety of authentic Mexican dishes alongside an equally strong drink selection. Despite the size of the menu, nothing about the experience felt unfocused or overwhelming. Instead, it showcased the restaurant’s versatility and commitment to offering something for every palate.

Service throughout the evening was excellent. Our waitress was exceptionally friendly, attentive, and welcoming without ever feeling intrusive. The staff contributed heavily to the relaxed and enjoyable energy of the restaurant, making the experience feel both comfortable and genuinely hospitable.

The food arrived beautifully presented and cooked with precision. Every dish was packed with bold, authentic flavor, with ingredients tasting fresh and thoughtfully prepared. The kitchen clearly understands balance and seasoning, delivering meals that felt both satisfying and carefully executed rather than rushed or overly commercialized.

The cocktail program deserves recognition as well. The drinks were creative, well-balanced, and expertly poured — visually appealing while still delivering on flavor. It’s evident that the bar program receives the same level of care and consistency as the kitchen.

What ultimately makes Mi Lindo San Blas stand out is its ability to combine strong food, excellent drinks, inviting atmosphere, and warm hospitality into one cohesive experience. In a dining landscape where many restaurants excel in one category while neglecting another, this establishment manages to deliver across the board.

Anyone in the Lincoln Park area looking for authentic Mexican cuisine in a vibrant and memorable setting should absolutely make time to visit Mi Lindo San Blas. My wife and I left thoroughly impressed and are already looking forward to returning.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: I live around the block from this place and have never gone until today (Cinco De Mayo) and let me tell you.... WHAT WAS I THINKING!?!??! This place is all that and a bag of chips. The menu was a little overwhelming but that is never a bad thing. Has great options for everyone and their drinks just taste better. I dont know if it is freshness or the care they made the drinks with but I was blow away! Please please please come in and let this place do the talking!

review_text: This was our first experience here, & it was wonderful. Not too busy, the dining room is clean and fun to look at.. Our server Fatima, was amazing. She let us take our time with the HUGE menu, was quick to ask us if we wanted a drink from the bar & got them while we browsed the options for food. 10/10 experience for a friday night dinner with my husband.

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text: Over all the best place I’ve been! I come back every other day when I get off work! I fell in love! I have to try something different I’m stunk on my three things but Fatima recommended a dish that I will try when I come back. Her service was great from the moment I walked into the door! Also she thought me how to pronounce my drink! I love that. Blessed! Again Thank you Fatima

location_name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
review_text:
I've adjusted my rating down to 1 star at this point.

After posting my original review, I received a response asking me to reach out so they could make things right. I did exactly that on June 3rd, and as of today, June 8th, I have not received any response or follow-up.

Unfortunately, the lack of communication and customer service has made it clear that our business is not valued. I sincerely hope you take steps to improve the experience for future guests; otherwise, you may continue to lose customers. To make matters worse, all of this stems from a $45 crab meal and one additional entrée. After leaving a generous tip, our total came to approximately $85. Based on this experience and the way the situation has been handled, we will not be ordering from this establishment again. Take care.

Update 5/31/2026
I've decided to update my review because I feel the quality of this restaurant has declined.

Ever since I first tried the crab legs, I've been a loyal customer, ordering them at least once a month. Up until now, they've been consistently good. This time, however, I placed an online order and was very disappointed. When I opened my meal, the sauce tasted completely different than usual. It was extremely oily, lacked flavor, and did not resemble what I've received in the past.

I also paid for extra cucumbers but received very few. Despite tipping well and spending approximately $85 on the order, the overall experience fell far short of expectations.

I called the restaurant right away and was told that the cooks prepared the meal the same way they always do. I explained that the taste was noticeably off, but my concerns weren't really addressed. I then spent several days trying to reach someone and wasn't able to speak with anyone until three days later.

At first, it seemed like the situation would be resolved with a replacement meal. Instead, I was offered a discount on a future order. As someone who has consistently supported this business, recommended it to friends and family, and returned month after month, being offered only a discount felt disappointing and dismissive.

I don't enjoy leaving negative reviews, especially for a place I've genuinely enjoyed for so long, but this experience left me feeling undervalued as a loyal customer.

3/6/26 Angel & Mayra make the best crab legs I have ever had. Omar & Fatima are always so kind, quick and attentive. Will come back again and again because of the quality of the food and the service here. Never been so comfortable in a restaurant! 😊

Update: Came up this evening, and Yasmine took care of us. She was amazing and so attentive. She knows what she is doing and gave us the best experience here!

Update: I came back to try the crab legs, and they did not disappoint. It was the most flavorful I've ever had! This is officially our new favorite place!

This place is gorgeous! Right when you walk in, you notice that it's colorful and well decorated. The bar is beautiful and lights up nice. A lot of wows walking in. Comfortable and warm environment with nice employees. Fabian was who served us, and he did great!  Didn't wait long for our food at all. The prices aren't bad whatsoever. There is so much to choose from on the menu. A lot of phenomenal looking drinks, too. I ordered the steak fajita quesadilla. 10/10 - by far, the best quesadilla I have ever had. My man had the carnitas. Those were outstanding, too! 10/10, everything tasted flavorful and perfect. Will most definitely be back to try the crab legs!! 🦀 Their cheese dip is pretty good, too. 10/10 You won't be disappointed coming to this place. We enjoyed our experience and will tell about it to all!
